hso_hl.objective: Asset inventories are established through a **documented entity-level asset inventory** with **identification + classification + documentation** of ICT supported business functions, information assets, ICT assets, and their roles and dependencies.
considerations: **3 of 5 regulations participate** (NIS 2, CRA, DORA — **GDPR + AI Act explicitly out-of-scope** per CRDA). NIS 2 Art. 21(2)(i) provides **partial coverage** via access-control coordination (asset-management is one of three coordinated sub-domains of access control); CRA Annex VII §1-§2 provides **partial coverage** (documentation-centric — general description + architecture description, not a formal asset register in the DORA sense); **DORA Art. 7(1) is the dedicated authority** with explicit OJ-literal `3-way AND` of ICT supported business functions + information assets + ICT assets + roles + dependencies.

**CRDA-deep verified (3 pairs: 1 SAME — COMPLEMENTARY NIS 2 ↔ CRA — substantively scope-disjoint per CRDA `N (typically)`, different objects (entity asset register vs. product architecture documentation), CRDA `complementary` is a taxonomy artifact; + 1 SAME — EQUAL NIS 2 ↔ DORA on entity-level asset inventory, DORA-dominant; + 1 SAME — COMPLEMENTARY CRA ↔ DORA, Conditional-Y when DORA entity procures CRA-conformant products)** — no CORRECTED, no CONTRADICTORY. The high-level SO does NOT collapse the entity-level asset register with the product-level architecture documentation.

**DORA sole-authority flag (per AEGIS taxonomy §4.1, OJ-literal)** distinguishes D-09.3 from D-07.2 where the sole-authority assignment is OJ-text-unsupported; NIS 2 and CRA provide partial coverage only.

**Scope caveats**: (a) NIS 2 ↔ CRA substantively scope-disjoint (entity asset register vs. product architecture documentation) — different audit purposes (entity-level NCA review vs. product-level MSA review), the two regimes meet only at the supplier-customer interface; (b) NIS 2 ↔ DORA EQUAL discharges both on the DORA-dominant stack for financial entities; (c) CRA ↔ DORA is DORA-led with CRA Annex VII §1-§2 as upstream product-level sub-register feeding the DORA entity-level master record.

The NIS2↔CRA pair is **substantively scope-disjoint per CRDA `N (typically)`** so it does **NOT** engage the high-level SO on its own — the high-level SO engages on the NIS2↔DORA EQUAL pair (DORA-dominant stack) and/or the CRA↔DORA COMPLEMENTARY pair (DORA-led stack with CRA Annex VII §1-§2 as upstream product sub-register). The high-level SO does NOT collapse any per-reg asset-inventory artefact; it surfaces the common entity-level asset-inventory duty with DORA Art. 7(1) as the dedicated authority for DORA financial entities and NIS 2 Art. 21(2)(i) + CRA Annex VII §1-§2 as partial-coverage inputs.

hso_per_reg.objective:
Essential and important entities adopt **access control policies coordinated with human resources security and asset management** (Art. 21(2)(i) NIS 2), with the management body approving, overseeing, and being liable for the cybersecurity risk-management measures (Art. 20(1) sentence 1 NIS 2).

**CSF anchors (from `SR-NIS2-005` v0.3 security_rationale):**
PR.PS-01 (primary), ID.AM-01, ID.AM-02.
*(PR.PS-01 — Configuration management practices established — anchors the NIS 2 Art. 21(2)(i) asset-management coordinated-with-access-control sub-domain mapped to NIST CSF 2.0 PR.PS-01 per CRDA DomainAnalysis §D-09.3 line 86 cross-validation; ID.AM-01 — Inventories of hardware managed within the system — and ID.AM-02 — Inventories of software, services, and systems managed — are the convergent NIST CSF 2.0 subcategories for the entity-level hardware + software + services + systems inventory per CRDA DomainAnalysis §D-09.3 line 86. The three subcategories together enable the NSIE to demonstrate ex post, against the Member State competent authority, that the Art. 21(2)(i) asset-management sub-domain — coordinated with access control — is established, reviewed, and updated in light of the proportionality factors (degree of exposure; likelihood of incidents; severity per Recital 56).)*

Manufacturers of products with digital elements draw up **technical documentation** containing at least a **general description** of the product (Annex VII §1) and a **description of the design, development, production and vulnerability-handling processes** including the system architecture description (Annex VII §2); the technical documentation is drawn up before placing on the market and updated during the support period (Art. 31(2) CRA), and kept at the disposal of market surveillance authorities for at least 10 years or the support period, whichever is longer (Art. 13(13) CRA).

**CSF anchors (from `SR-CRA-087` + `SR-CRA-088` v0.3 security_rationale):**
ID.AM-01, ID.AM-02.
*(ID.AM-01 — Inventories of hardware managed within the system — and ID.AM-02 — Inventories of software, services, and systems managed — are the convergent NIST CSF 2.0 subcategories per CRDA DomainAnalysis §D-09.3 line 86 cross-validation; the CRA Annex VII §1-§2 mandate covers the software-versions + system-architecture dimensions of these subcategories at the product level (intended purpose + software versions affecting compliance + architecture description). The two subcategories together enable the manufacturer to demonstrate ex post, against the market surveillance authority, that the Annex VII §1-§2 general description + architecture description were drawn up before placing on the market (Art. 31(2)) and kept up to date during the support period with the 10-year retention per Art. 13(13). **IMPORTANT — CRA partial coverage**: CRA Annex VII §1-§2 is documentation-centric (general description + architecture), not a formal asset register in the DORA sense; the partial coverage does not substitute for the NIS 2 entity-level asset-management sub-domain (substantively scope-disjoint per CRDA `N (typically)`) or the DORA entity-level `3-way AND` inventory (different audit purposes — product-level MSA review vs. entity-level NCA review). **CRA presumption of conformity per Art. 27(1)/(2)/(5)/(8)/(9)** discharges the CRA conformity-assessment obligation only; it does not substitute for the NIS 2 entity-level asset-management sub-domain or the DORA Art. 7(1) `3-way AND` inventory obligation.)*

Financial entities **identify, classify and adequately document all ICT supported business functions, the roles and responsibilities supporting those functions, the information assets and ICT assets supporting those functions, and their roles and dependencies in relation to ICT risk** (Art. 7(1) DORA — the dedicated authority with explicit OJ-literal `3-way AND`); responsibility for managing and overseeing ICT risk is assigned to a control function with appropriate independence per the three lines of defence model (Art. 6(4) DORA).

**CSF anchors (from `SR-DORA-029` v0.3 security_rationale):**
ID.AM-01, ID.AM-02, ID.AM-05.
*(ID.AM-01 — Inventories of hardware managed within the system — anchors the DORA Art. 7(1) `information assets` + `ICT assets` dimensions mapped to NIST CSF 2.0 ID.AM-01; ID.AM-02 — Inventories of software, services, and systems managed — anchors the DORA Art. 7(1) `ICT supported business functions` + software-inventory dimensions; ID.AM-05 — Assets are prioritized based on classification, criticality, and business value — anchors the DORA Art. 7(1) `classify` dimension per CRDA DomainAnalysis §D-09.3 line 86 cross-validation. The three subcategories together (ID.AM-01 + ID.AM-02 + ID.AM-05) are the convergent NIST CSF 2.0 subcategories for DORA's entity-level asset inventory with the **classification dimension unique to DORA** (NIS 2 does not impose the classify dimension; CRA does not impose the classify dimension). The three subcategories together enable the financial entity to demonstrate ex post, against the national competent authority + EBA supervisory baseline, that the Art. 7(1) `3-way AND` ICT asset register + Art. 6(4) three-lines-of-defence were established, reviewed, and updated in light of the proportionality factors (size, nature, scale, complexity per Art. 4). For DORA financial entities that are also NSIEs, the DORA Art. 7(1) `3-way AND` discharges both the NIS 2 partial-coverage asset-management dimension and DORA's dedicated Art. 7(1) obligation — **DORA is the dedicated authority with OJ-literal `3-way AND`** distinct from the D-07.2 OJ-text-unsupported sole-authority assignment.)*

## 1. Cross-Regulation Analysis

> **Domain analysis:** [D-09.3 Asset Inventories](../../DomainAnalysis/D-09_Governance-Documentation/D-09.3.md)

### D-09.3 Asset Inventories

<!-- participants: NIS2, CRA, DORA -->

**Participants (from CRDA):** NIS2, CRA, DORA

**Sole-authority note (CRDA flag):** DORA Art. 7(1) is the dedicated authority — explicit `identify, classify, adequately document` mandate on the `3-way AND` of ICT supported business functions + information assets + ICT assets. NIS 2 and CRA provide partial coverage only.

#### Pair: NIS2 ↔ CRA

**NIS2 article (verbatim, Art. 21(2)(i)):**
> "[Member States shall ensure that essential and important entities take appropriate and proportionate technical, operational and organisational measures to … ] (i) human resources security, access control policies and asset management." — Art. 21(2)(i) NIS 2 (per SR-NIS2-005)

**CRA article (verbatim, Annex VII §1-§2):**
> "The technical documentation shall contain: (1) a general description of the product with digital elements … (2) a description of the design, development, production and vulnerability-handling processes of the product with digital elements …" — CRA Annex VII §1-§2 (per SR-CRA-087)

**Scope comparison:**
- NIS 2 scope: asset management as one of three coordinated sub-domains of access control (Art. 21(2)(i) — partial coverage).
- CRA scope: general description + architecture documentation (Annex VII §1-§2 — documentation-centric, not a formal asset register).
- Scope overlap: **N (typically)** — different objects (entity asset register vs. product architecture documentation).

**Trigger comparison:**
- NIS 2 trigger: entity asset management.
- CRA trigger: manufacturer technical-documentation obligation.
- Trigger alignment: **DIFFERENT**.

**Threshold/timeline comparison:**
- NIS 2: no quantitative cadence.
- CRA: support period + 10-year accessibility.
- Tension: **NONE**.

**Recipient comparison:**
- NIS 2: management body, competent authority.
- CRA: MSA.

**Content template comparison:**
- NIS 2: not codified for granularity.
- CRA: general description + architecture.

**Classified relationship (from CRDA):** COMPLEMENTARY
**Verified relationship (after OJ-text analysis):** **SAME — COMPLEMENTARY**
**Reasoning:** NIS 2 covers entity-level asset management; CRA covers product-level architecture documentation. The two regimes meet at the supplier-customer interface — CRA Annex VII §1-§2 architecture documentation feeds the NIS 2 entity's asset register.

**Scope-disjoint test:** Does this pair actually overlap on a single event at a single party? **N (typically)** — different objects (entity asset register vs. product architecture documentation). Substantively scope-disjoint; CRDA "complementary" is a taxonomy artifact.

**Downstream implication (Phase 1C Doc 07):** Phase 2 obligation derivation must produce two parallel artefacts: NIS 2 Art. 21(2)(i) asset-management sub-domain + CRA Annex VII §1-§2 architecture documentation as upstream input.

---

#### Pair: NIS2 ↔ DORA

**NIS2 article (verbatim, Art. 21(2)(i)):**
> [as quoted above]

**DORA article (verbatim, Art. 7(1) per SR-DORA-029; OJ-literal locus Art. 8(1) per the v0.2 audit):**
> "Financial entities shall identify, classify and adequately document all ICT supported business functions, the roles and responsibilities supporting those functions, the information assets and ICT assets supporting those functions, and their roles and dependencies in relation to ICT risk." — DORA Art. 7(1) per SR-DORA-029

**Scope comparison:**
- NIS 2 scope: asset management as one of three coordinated sub-domains of access control (Art. 21(2)(i) — partial coverage).
- DORA scope: financial-entity identify, classify, document mandate on ICT supported business functions + information assets + ICT assets + roles + dependencies (the dedicated authority per AEGIS taxonomy §4.1).
- Scope overlap: **Y (when a DORA financial entity is also an NSIE).**

**Trigger comparison:**
- NIS 2 trigger: entity asset management.
- DORA trigger: financial-entity ICT risk identification (Art. 7(1)).
- Trigger alignment: **SAME** (both entity-level asset inventory).

**Threshold/timeline comparison:**
- NIS 2: no quantitative cadence.
- DORA: no quantitative cadence.
- Tension: **NONE**.

**Recipient comparison:**
- NIS 2: management body, competent authority.
- DORA: NCA.

**Content template comparison:**
- NIS 2: not codified for granularity.
- DORA: 3-way AND of ICT supported business functions + information assets + ICT assets + roles + dependencies.

**Classified relationship (from CRDA):** EQUAL
**Verified relationship (after OJ-text analysis):** **SAME — EQUAL**
**Reasoning:** Both regulations impose asset-inventory obligations. DORA Art. 7(1) is the dominant authority with explicit OJ-literal anchor. NIS 2 Art. 21(2)(i) is partial via access-control coordination.

**Scope-disjoint test:** Does this pair actually overlap on a single event at a single party? **Y (when the DORA financial entity is also an NSIE applying asset inventory)**. No reclassification required.

**Downstream implication (Phase 1C Doc 07):** Phase 2 obligation derivation must produce a DORA-dominant stack: DORA Art. 7(1) explicit `identify, classify, adequately document` mandate discharges both DORA's dedicated inventory obligation and NIS 2's Art. 21(2)(i) partial-coverage asset-management sub-domain for DORA financial entities.

---

#### Pair: CRA ↔ DORA

**CRA article (verbatim, Annex VII §1-§2):**
> [as quoted above]

**DORA article (verbatim, Art. 7(1) per SR-DORA-029; OJ-literal locus Art. 8(1) per the v0.2 audit):**
> [as quoted above]

**Scope comparison:**
- CRA scope: general description + architecture documentation (Annex VII §1-§2 — documentation-centric, not a formal asset register).
- DORA scope: financial-entity identify, classify, document mandate.
- Scope overlap: **Conditional (Y when the DORA entity procures CRA-conformant products)**.

**Trigger comparison:**
- CRA trigger: manufacturer technical-documentation obligation.
- DORA trigger: financial-entity ICT risk identification.
- Trigger alignment: **DIFFERENT**.

**Threshold/timeline comparison:**
- CRA: support period + 10-year accessibility.
- DORA: no quantitative cadence.
- Tension: **NONE**.

**Recipient comparison:**
- CRA: MSA.
- DORA: NCA.

**Content template comparison:**
- CRA: general description + architecture.
- DORA: 3-way AND inventory.

**Classified relationship (from CRDA):** COMPLEMENTARY
**Verified relationship (after OJ-text analysis):** **SAME — COMPLEMENTARY**
**Reasoning:** CRA architecture documentation feeds the DORA entity's asset register. DORA's ICT asset inventory is the entity-level master record; CRA Annex VII §1-§2 is the product-level sub-register.

**Scope-disjoint test:** Does this pair actually overlap on a single event at a single party? **Conditional (Y when the DORA entity procures CRA-conformant products; N otherwise)**. No reclassification required.

**Downstream implication (Phase 1C Doc 07):** Phase 2 obligation derivation must produce a DORA-led stack: DORA Art. 7(1) master record + CRA Annex VII §1-§2 product-level sub-register.

### Participants (from CRDA-deep §D-09.3)

| Regulation | Canonical SO | 1-line summary | Scope/angle |
|---|---|---|---|
| NIS2 | SO-NIS2-003 (primary, partial) + SO-NIS2-021 (cross) | Entity-level asset-management sub-domain coordinated with access control (Art. 21(2)(i) `human resources security, access control policies and asset management`) + management-body oversight of Art. 21 measures (Art. 20(1)). | Partial coverage via access-control coordination; no dedicated asset-inventory mandate; NIS 2 does not impose a dedicated asset-inventory mandate (NIS 2 participant note: "NIS 2 does not impose a dedicated asset-inventory mandate"). |
| CRA | SO-CRA-061 (partial) | Annex VII §1 `general description` (intended purpose + software versions affecting compliance) + Annex VII §2 `design, development, production and vulnerability-handling processes` (system architecture description documenting how software components build on or feed into each other). | Documentation-centric, not a formal asset register in the DORA sense; **partial coverage only**; **substantively scope-disjoint from NIS 2** per CRDA `N (typically)` (per CRDA-deep `§D-09.3 line 66`). |
| DORA | SO-DORA-017 (sole authority) | Identify, classify, and adequately document ICT supported business functions + roles and responsibilities + information assets + ICT assets + their roles and dependencies in relation to ICT risk (Art. 7(1)). | Entity-level formal asset inventory; **the dedicated authority per AEGIS taxonomy §4.1** (OJ-literal Art. 7(1) 3-way AND); DORA-dominant for DORA financial entities. |
| GDPR | (out-of-scope as strict mandate) | RoPA focus on processing purposes, data categories, recipients, safeguards — not information assets. No standalone SO for D-09.3 from GDPR. | RoPA is a related-but-distinct artefact (see D-09.4). |
| AI_Act | (out-of-scope) | AI Act does not impose an asset-inventory mandate. | — |

### Validated relationships (from CRDA-deep §D-09.3)

Of 3 pairs, **3 verified as SAME — no CORRECTED, no CONTRADICTORY**. The 3 verified relationships are distributed as **1 SAME — COMPLEMENTARY (NIS 2 ↔ CRA — substantively scope-disjoint per CRDA `N (typically)`) + 1 SAME — EQUAL (NIS 2 ↔ DORA — DORA-dominant for financial entities) + 1 SAME — COMPLEMENTARY (CRA ↔ DORA — Conditional-Y)**. **The three participating regulators look at the asset-inventory duty from three distinct regulatory perspectives**, with DORA Art. 7(1) as the dedicated OJ-literal authority for entity-level asset-inventory; NIS 2 Art. 21(2)(i) + CRA Annex VII §1-§2 provide partial coverage that **does not collapse into the DORA-dominant stack**:

- **NIS2 ↔ CRA** — COMPLEMENTARY (SAME) **with critical CRDA scope-disjoint flag**: NIS 2 covers **entity-level asset management** via Art. 21(2)(i) `human resources security, access control policies and asset management` (partial coverage, one of three coordinated sub-domains of access control); CRA covers **product-level architecture documentation** via Annex VII §1 `general description` + Annex VII §2 `design, development, production and vulnerability-handling processes` (partial coverage, documentation-centric). **Scope-disjoint test: `N (typically)`** — different objects (entity asset register vs. product architecture documentation); substantively scope-disjoint. Per CRDA-deep `§D-09.3 line 66`: "CRDA `complementary` is a taxonomy artifact". The two regimes meet at the **supplier-customer interface** — CRA Annex VII §1-§2 architecture documentation (per SR-CRA-087) feeds the NIS 2 entity's asset register (per SR-NIS2-005). No reclassification required. The high-level SO does **NOT** collapse the two artefacts because they serve different audit purposes.
- **NIS2 ↔ DORA** — EQUAL (SAME): Both regulations impose **asset-inventory obligations** at the entity level. NIS 2: Art. 21(2)(i) `human resources security, access control policies and asset management` (partial via access-control coordination; entity-discretionary under Recital 56 proportionality per SR-NIS2-005). DORA: Art. 7(1) `Financial entities shall identify, classify and adequately document all ICT supported business functions, the roles and responsibilities supporting those functions, the information assets and ICT assets supporting those functions, and their roles and dependencies in relation to ICT risk` (sole authority, the dedicated OJ-literal anchor). **DORA Art. 7(1) is the dominant authority** for DORA financial entities; the explicit OJ-literal `identify, classify and adequately document` mandate on the `3-way AND` of ICT supported business functions + information assets + ICT assets + roles + dependencies is more prescriptive than the NIS 2 partial-coordination approach. For DORA financial entities, the DORA entity-level asset inventory discharges both DORA's dedicated Art. 7(1) obligation and NIS 2's Art. 21(2)(i) partial-coverage asset-management dimension (cross-coupled via access-control). DORA Art. 7(1) is the operationalised identifier; **CRDA flag** per CRDA-deep `§D-09.3 line 105`: "DORA Art. 7(1) is the dominant authority with explicit OJ-literal anchor. NIS 2 Art. 21(2)(i) is partial via access-control coordination." Scope-disjoint test: **Y (when a DORA financial entity is also an NSIE)**. No reclassification required.
- **CRA ↔ DORA** — COMPLEMENTARY (SAME): CRA architecture documentation feeds the DORA entity's asset register. CRA scope: Annex VII §1-§2 general description + architecture documentation (documentation-centric). DORA scope: Art. 7(1) `identify, classify, adequately document` (the dedicated authority; entity-level master record). **DORA's ICT asset inventory is the entity-level master record; CRA Annex VII §1-§2 is the product-level sub-register** — the two artefacts operate on different layers (CRA on the product layer; DORA on the entity layer). Per CRDA-deep `§D-09.3 line 146`: "CRA architecture documentation feeds the DORA entity's asset register. DORA's ICT asset inventory is the entity-level master record; CRA Annex VII §1-§2 is the product-level sub-register." Scope-disjoint test: **Conditional (Y when the DORA entity procures CRA-conformant products; N otherwise)**. No reclassification required. The DORA-led stack with CRA Annex VII §1-§2 as upstream product-level sub-register.

The asset-inventory architecture is **legitimately diverse with DORA Art. 7(1) sole-authority + NIS 2 + CRA partial coverage**. The three regulators impose asset-inventory artefacts from three distinct regulatory perspectives under three distinct anchors: **NIS 2 Art. 21(2)(i) entity-level partial asset-management coordination** (with access control as the converging sub-domain; asset-management is one of three coordinated sub-domains of access control); **CRA Annex VII §1-§2 product-level partial architecture documentation** (documentation-centric; the general description and architecture description of how software components build on or feed into each other); **DORA Art. 7(1) financial-entity dedicated asset inventory** (the dedicated authority; explicit OJ-literal `3-way AND` of ICT supported business functions + information assets + ICT assets + roles + dependencies). **The CRDA substantively scope-disjoint flag on NIS 2 ↔ CRA is structurally significant** — the pair operates on different objects (entity asset register vs. product architecture documentation) serving different audit purposes; no OJ-level consolidation rule exists between NIS 2 and CRA for the asset-inventory object. The high-level SO does **NOT** collapse the entity-level asset register with the product-level architecture documentation; the two artefacts stack on the supplier-customer interface.

The scope-overlap column in the matrix below indicates whether the same event can trigger both regulations on a single party; this is the gating condition for the high-level SO to engage.

| | NIS2 | CRA | DORA |
|---|---|---|---|
| **NIS2** | — | **N (typically)** — different objects (entity asset register vs. product architecture documentation); substantively scope-disjoint per CRDA; CRDA `complementary` is a taxonomy artifact; the two regimes meet at the supplier-customer interface (CRA-conformant product is an input to the NIS 2 entity's asset register) | Y (DORA financial entity is also an NSIE; DORA Art. 7(1) `identify, classify, adequately document` is the dominant authority with explicit OJ-literal anchor — DORA-dominant stack discharges both DORA's dedicated Art. 7(1) obligation and NIS 2's Art. 21(2)(i) partial-coverage asset-management dimension — EQUAL on entity-level asset inventory) |

Legend: **Y** = scope overlap on a single event at a single party (high-level SO engages on the joint-condition operator). **N (typically)** = substantively scope-disjoint per CRDA — no same-event overlap (high-level SO does **NOT** engage on this pair; CRDA `complementary` is a taxonomy artifact). **Conditional (Y/N)** = conditional overlap depending on entity-product combination — Y when the listed entity-product combination applies on a single party, N otherwise. CRDA-deep scope-disjoint tests: NIS2↔CRA = **N (typically)** — substantively scope-disjoint; NIS2↔DORA = Y; CRA↔DORA = Conditional (Y when DORA entity procures CRA-conformant products).

### Cross-recipient map

D-09.3 generates a layered recipient set on the **asset-inventory evidence trail** but does **not generate a notification cascade of D-09.3 itself**. The recipient set is at the **asset-inventory-evidence layer**: **NIS 2's management body + competent authority** on the Art. 21(2)(i) `human resources security, access control policies and asset management` asset-management sub-domain + Art. 20(1) management-body approval records (cross-coupled with the policy-architecture requirement at D-09.1); **CRA's market surveillance authority (MSA)** on the Annex VII §1 `general description` + Annex VII §2 `design, development, production and vulnerability-handling processes` (per SR-CRA-087) + 10-year technical-documentation retention per Art. 13(13) (cross to D-09.4) + conformity-assessment records per Art. 27 + Article 31(2) `drawn up before placing on the market and updated during the support period`; **DORA's competent authority (NCA — national financial supervisory authority)** on the Art. 7(1) `identify, classify, adequately document` ICT asset register + Art. 6(4) three-lines-of-defence + EBA Guidelines on ICT and security risk management supervisory baseline. These are distinct regulatory channels on the same asset-inventory artefacts but they do not generate a notification cascade of D-09.3 itself — they are **compliance-evidence channels on the layered asset-inventory architecture**. GDPR's DPA is **NOT** a recipient because GDPR is explicitly out-of-scope for D-09.3 (RoPA is a related-but-distinct artefact at D-09.4). The AI Act MSA + AI Office is **NOT** a recipient because the AI Act is explicitly out-of-scope for D-09.3 (no asset-inventory mandate). The **D-09.1 (Information Security Policies)** is the upstream policy artefact where the asset-inventory architecture is governed by the documented policies; **D-09.4 (Records of Processing)** is the documentation-evidence downstream artefact where the asset register itself is one of the records to be retained (CRA Annex VII §1-§2 technical documentation; DORA ICT asset register documentation; NIS 2 entity-discretionary record). **D-09.2 (Impact & Risk Assessments)** is the operational downstream artefact where asset-classification feeds the entity-risk register. **D-06.1 (Vendor Risk Assessment)** is the supplier-side downstream where the CRA Annex VII §1-§2 architecture documentation (via the NIS 2 supplier-customer interface) is an input to the NIS 2 entity's supplier-risk register (cross-coupled via access control).

**Objective.** Asset inventories are established through a **documented entity-level asset inventory** with **identification + classification + documentation** of ICT supported business functions, information assets, ICT assets, and their roles and dependencies.

**Considerations.**
- **3 of 5 regulations participate** (NIS 2, CRA, DORA — **GDPR + AI Act explicitly out-of-scope** per CRDA). NIS 2 Art. 21(2)(i) provides **partial coverage** via access-control coordination (asset-management is one of three coordinated sub-domains of access control); CRA Annex VII §1-§2 provides **partial coverage** (documentation-centric — general description + architecture description, not a formal asset register in the DORA sense); **DORA Art. 7(1) is the dedicated authority** with explicit OJ-literal `3-way AND` of ICT supported business functions + information assets + ICT assets + roles + dependencies.
- **CRDA-deep verified (3 pairs: 1 SAME — COMPLEMENTARY NIS 2 ↔ CRA — substantively scope-disjoint per CRDA `N (typically)`, different objects (entity asset register vs. product architecture documentation), CRDA `complementary` is a taxonomy artifact; + 1 SAME — EQUAL NIS 2 ↔ DORA on entity-level asset inventory, DORA-dominant; + 1 SAME — COMPLEMENTARY CRA ↔ DORA, Conditional-Y when DORA entity procures CRA-conformant products)** — no CORRECTED, no CONTRADICTORY. The high-level SO does NOT collapse the entity-level asset register with the product-level architecture documentation.
- **DORA sole-authority flag (per AEGIS taxonomy §4.1, OJ-literal)** distinguishes D-09.3 from D-07.2 where the sole-authority assignment is OJ-text-unsupported; NIS 2 and CRA provide partial coverage only.
- **Scope caveats**: (a) NIS 2 ↔ CRA substantively scope-disjoint (entity asset register vs. product architecture documentation) — different audit purposes (entity-level NCA review vs. product-level MSA review), the two regimes meet only at the supplier-customer interface; (b) NIS 2 ↔ DORA EQUAL discharges both on the DORA-dominant stack for financial entities; (c) CRA ↔ DORA is DORA-led with CRA Annex VII §1-§2 as upstream product-level sub-register feeding the DORA entity-level master record.

```yaml
verified_relationship: PARTIAL (CRDA-deep, D-09.3: NIS2↔CRA = SAME — COMPLEMENTARY (substantively scope-disjoint per CRDA `N (typically)` — different objects, entity asset register vs. product architecture documentation; substantively scope-disjoint; CRDA `complementary` is a taxonomy artifact); NIS2↔DORA = SAME — EQUAL (DORA Art. 7(1) sole authority; DORA-dominant for financial entities); CRA↔DORA = SAME — COMPLEMENTARY (Conditional-Y when DORA entity procures CRA-conformant products; CRA Annex VII §1-§2 architecture documentation feeds DORA asset register; DORA-led stack with CRA Annex VII §1-§2 as upstream product-level sub-register))
```

**Objective.** Manufacturers of products with digital elements draw up **technical documentation** containing at least a **general description** of the product (Annex VII §1) and a **description of the design, development, production and vulnerability-handling processes** including the system architecture description (Annex VII §2); the technical documentation is drawn up before placing on the market and updated during the support period (Art. 31(2) CRA), and kept at the disposal of market surveillance authorities for at least 10 years or the support period, whichever is longer (Art. 13(13) CRA).

**CSF anchors (from `SR-CRA-087` + `SR-CRA-088` v0.3 security_rationale):**
ID.AM-01, ID.AM-02.
*(ID.AM-01 — Inventories of hardware managed within the system — and ID.AM-02 — Inventories of software, services, and systems managed — are the convergent NIST CSF 2.0 subcategories per CRDA DomainAnalysis §D-09.3 line 86 cross-validation; the CRA Annex VII §1-§2 mandate covers the software-versions + system-architecture dimensions of these subcategories at the product level (intended purpose + software versions affecting compliance + architecture description). The two subcategories together enable the manufacturer to demonstrate ex post, against the market surveillance authority, that the Annex VII §1-§2 general description + architecture description were drawn up before placing on the market (Art. 31(2)) and kept up to date during the support period with the 10-year retention per Art. 13(13). **IMPORTANT — CRA partial coverage**: CRA Annex VII §1-§2 is documentation-centric (general description + architecture), not a formal asset register in the DORA sense; the partial coverage does not substitute for the NIS 2 entity-level asset-management sub-domain (substantively scope-disjoint per CRDA `N (typically)`) or the DORA entity-level `3-way AND` inventory (different audit purposes — product-level MSA review vs. entity-level NCA review). **CRA presumption of conformity per Art. 27(1)/(2)/(5)/(8)/(9)** discharges the CRA conformity-assessment obligation only; it does not substitute for the NIS 2 entity-level asset-management sub-domain or the DORA Art. 7(1) `3-way AND` inventory obligation.)*

---

### D-09.3.3 — Sub-SO for DORA

```yaml
id: SO-D-09.3.DORA
subdomain: D-09.3
applies_to: [DORA]
inherits_from: SO-DORA-017 (sole authority per AEGIS taxonomy §4.1)
source_SR: SR-DORA-029
activation: if DORA in applicable_regs
phase_1A_role: 3_of_3
verified_relationship: PARTIAL (CRDA-deep, D-09.3: NIS2↔DORA = SAME — EQUAL (DORA Art. 7(1) sole authority with explicit OJ-literal anchor; both entity-level asset inventory; DORA-dominant for financial entities under DORA's direct scope); NIS2↔CRA = SAME — COMPLEMENTARY (substantively scope-disjoint per CRDA `N (typically)`); CRA↔DORA = SAME — COMPLEMENTARY (Conditional-Y when DORA entity procures CRA-conformant products; CRA Annex VII §1-§2 architecture documentation feeds DORA asset register; DORA-led stack with CRA Annex VII §1-§2 as upstream product-level sub-register))
```

**Objective.** Financial entities **identify, classify and adequately document all ICT supported business functions, the roles and responsibilities supporting those functions, the information assets and ICT assets supporting those functions, and their roles and dependencies in relation to ICT risk** (Art. 7(1) DORA — the dedicated authority with explicit OJ-literal `3-way AND`); responsibility for managing and overseeing ICT risk is assigned to a control function with appropriate independence per the three lines of defence model (Art. 6(4) DORA).

**CSF anchors (from `SR-DORA-029` v0.3 security_rationale):**
ID.AM-01, ID.AM-02, ID.AM-05.
*(ID.AM-01 — Inventories of hardware managed within the system — anchors the DORA Art. 7(1) `information assets` + `ICT assets` dimensions mapped to NIST CSF 2.0 ID.AM-01; ID.AM-02 — Inventories of software, services, and systems managed — anchors the DORA Art. 7(1) `ICT supported business functions` + software-inventory dimensions; ID.AM-05 — Assets are prioritized based on classification, criticality, and business value — anchors the DORA Art. 7(1) `classify` dimension per CRDA DomainAnalysis §D-09.3 line 86 cross-validation. The three subcategories together (ID.AM-01 + ID.AM-02 + ID.AM-05) are the convergent NIST CSF 2.0 subcategories for DORA's entity-level asset inventory with the **classification dimension unique to DORA** (NIS 2 does not impose the classify dimension; CRA does not impose the classify dimension). The three subcategories together enable the financial entity to demonstrate ex post, against the national competent authority + EBA supervisory baseline, that the Art. 7(1) `3-way AND` ICT asset register + Art. 6(4) three-lines-of-defence were established, reviewed, and updated in light of the proportionality factors (size, nature, scale, complexity per Art. 4). For DORA financial entities that are also NSIEs, the DORA Art. 7(1) `3-way AND` discharges both the NIS 2 partial-coverage asset-management dimension and DORA's dedicated Art. 7(1) obligation — **DORA is the dedicated authority with OJ-literal `3-way AND`** distinct from the D-07.2 OJ-text-unsupported sole-authority assignment.)*

---

### Emergent tensions (from CRDA-deep §D-09.3)

**0 conditional emergent tensions** on the asset-inventory duty itself — no pair was verified as CONTRADICTORY or CORRECTED. The 3 pairs are all SAME — 1 SAME — COMPLEMENTARY + 1 SAME — EQUAL + 1 SAME — COMPLEMENTARY. **No CORTRADICTORY or CORRECTED reclassifications** — CRDA preserves every pair as SAME.

The **DORA sole-authority flag (CRDA DomainAnalysis §D-09.3 line 80)** is the structurally significant operational divergence — DORA Art. 7(1) is the dedicated authority per AEGIS taxonomy §4.1 with explicit OJ-literal `3-way AND` of ICT supported business functions + information assets + ICT assets + roles + dependencies; NIS 2 and CRA provide partial coverage only. **This distinguishes D-09.3 from D-07.2** where the sole-authority assignment is OJ-text-unsupported. Phase 1 entities with DORA applicability must maintain the DORA-compliant entity-level master record (Art. 7(1)) as the entity-level asset register. For DORA financial entities that are also NSIEs, the DORA Art. 7(1) `3-way AND` discharges both regulations on the EQUAL pair. For DORA financial entities that procure CRA-conformant products, the CRA Annex VII §1-§2 architecture documentation is the upstream product-level sub-register feeding the DORA entity-level master record on the COMPLEMENTARY pair.

The **substantive scope-disjoint flag on NIS 2 ↔ CRA (CRDA-deep §D-09.3 line 66)** is the structurally significant operational divergence — the pair operates on **different objects** (entity asset register vs. product architecture documentation) serving **different audit purposes** (entity-level NCA review vs. product-level MSA review). CRDA `complementary` is a taxonomy artifact. The two regimes meet at the **supplier-customer interface** only (CRA-conformant product is an input to the NIS 2 entity's asset register). No OJ-level cross-referencing exists between NIS 2 and CRA for the asset-inventory object. Phase 1 entities with NIS 2 + CRA applicability (non-DORA manufacturer + NSIE) must discharge both obligations separately on distinct artefacts that are not substitutable.

The **NIS 2 ↔ DORA EQUAL classification on entity-level asset inventory** is the structurally significant EQUAL pair: both regulations impose entity-level asset-inventory obligations (NIS 2 Art. 21(2)(i) partial via access-control coordination; DORA Art. 7(1) explicit `3-way AND`). **DORA Art. 7(1) is more prescriptive than NIS 2 Art. 21(2)(i)** — the dedicated authority with the `3-way AND` of ICT supported business functions + information assets + ICT assets + roles + dependencies. For DORA financial entities, the DORA Art. 7(1) entity-level `3-way AND` inventory discharges both DORA's dedicated Art. 7(1) obligation and NIS 2's Art. 21(2)(i) partial-coverage asset-management dimension — DORA-dominant under DORA's direct scope (EQUAL classification). For non-financial NSIEs, the NIS 2 Art. 21(2)(i) partial-coverage asset-management sub-domain stands alone (no DORA Art. 7(1) obligation).

The **CRA ↔ DORA COMPLEMENTARY pair on DORA-led stack** is the structurally significant COMPLEMENTARY pair: CRA Annex VII §1-§2 architecture documentation feeds the DORA entity-level asset register; DORA's ICT asset inventory is the entity-level master record; CRA Annex VII §1-§2 is the product-level sub-register. The two artefacts operate on different layers (CRA on the product layer; DORA on the entity layer). The DORA-led stack with CRA Annex VII §1-§2 as upstream product-level sub-register is operationalised at the supplier-customer interface; no OJ-level cross-referencing exists between CRA and DORA for the asset-inventory object.

The high-level SO surfaces the **common entity-level asset-inventory duty** (identification + classification + documentation of the `3-way AND` of ICT supported business functions + information assets + ICT assets + roles + dependencies) with **DORA Art. 7(1) as the dedicated authority** for DORA financial entities and NIS 2 Art. 21(2)(i) + CRA Annex VII §1-§2 as partial-coverage inputs serving distinct audit purposes (entity-level NCA review, product-level MSA review, supplier-customer interface for cross-layer integration). The per-reg perspective on the asset-inventory artefact, anchor, governance body, content template, threshold, recipient, and evidence template is documented separately in the 3 sub-SOs and the per-recipient evidence packs. **0 conditional emergent tensions** because no pair was verified as CONTRADICTORY or CORRECTED — the 3-distinct-artefacts structure (NIS 2 partial via access control / CRA partial via Annex VII §1-§2 / DORA Art. 7(1) dedicated authority) with the substantive scope-disjoint flag on NIS 2 ↔ CRA is itself the structural reconciliation that supersedes any single-regulator reading.

### Downstream implication for Phase 1C Doc 07

A company with ≥2 applicable regulations in scope of D-09.3 — with overlapping asset-inventory scope-determiners on the same party — operates a **layered 3-artefact asset-inventory architecture** with **DORA Art. 7(1) as the dedicated authority** (when DORA applies) and partial-coverage inputs from NIS 2 Art. 21(2)(i) (entity-level partial asset-management coordination via access control) + CRA Annex VII §1-§2 (product-level partial architecture documentation). The high-level SO (`SO-D-09.3.HL`) describes the **common entity-level asset-inventory duty** (identification + classification + documentation of the `3-way AND` of ICT supported business functions + information assets + ICT assets + roles + dependencies) with DORA Art. 7(1) as the dedicated authority; the per-reg perspective is preserved in the 3 sub-SOs (artefact, governance body, anchor, content template, threshold, recipient, evidence template). **The 0 conditional emergent tensions are documented as a layered reconciliation**, with the 3-artefact structure operationalising the per-reg governance body on the integrated asset-inventory architecture. The high-level SO does NOT collapse the 3 asset-inventory artefacts (NIS 2 partial via access control / CRA partial via Annex VII §1-§2 / DORA Art. 7(1) dedicated authority); the per-reg perspective on the artefact, governance body, anchor, content template, threshold, recipient, and evidence template is preserved verbatim. **The NIS 2 ↔ CRA substantive scope-disjoint flag** means the two artefacts cannot be cross-substituted; they coexist on the supplier-customer interface. Phase 1C Doc 07 documents the **asset-inventory strategy**: (1) a DORA-dominant stack for financial entities with the DORA Art. 7(1) `3-way AND` inventory as the entity-level master record (per SR-DORA-029) and the CRA Annex VII §1-§2 architecture documentation as upstream product-level sub-register (per SR-CRA-087) when CRA-conformant products are procured (Conditional-Y scope overlap); (2) the **DORA sole-authority OJ-supported flag** distinguishing D-09.3 from D-07.2 (OJ-literal `3-way AND`); (3) the **NIS 2 ↔ CRA substantive scope-disjoint flag** preserved as a Layer 1 cross-cutting architecture-mapping item (no OJ-level consolidation; entity asset register + product architecture documentation on the supplier-customer interface); (4) the **NIS 2 ↔ DORA EQUAL pair** discharges both regulations on the DORA-dominant stack for financial entities under DORA's direct scope; (5) the **CRA ↔ DORA COMPLEMENTARY pair** stacks the CRA Annex VII §1-§2 as upstream product sub-register feeding the DORA entity-level master record (DORA-led stack); (6) **GDPR RoPA is explicitly out-of-scope** for D-09.3 per CRDA explicit note (RoPA is a related-but-distinct artefact at D-09.4, not a strict asset-inventory mandate); (7) **AI Act is explicitly out-of-scope** for D-09.3 per CRDA explicit note (no asset-inventory mandate). The Layer 1 mapping reconciles the 3 asset-inventory artefacts (NIS 2 + CRA + DORA) on the supplier-customer interface (CRA → NIS 2/DORA upstream; DORA Art. 7(1) entity-level master record for financial entities) without OJ-level consolidation.

> **Provenance.** Derived from `../../HierarchicalSecurityObjectives/D-09_Governance-Documentation/9.3.md`. This file adds Volere shell fields per the `../Volere_shell.md` template.

## Participating regulations

NIS 2, CRA, DORA (**3 of 5 regulations**; **GDPR + AI Act explicitly out-of-scope** per CRDA: GDPR RoPA focus is on processing purposes / data categories / recipients / safeguards — not information assets (RoPA is a related-but-distinct artefact at 9.4); AI Act does not impose an asset-inventory mandate). Each regulator imposes a **distinct asset-inventory artefact** at a **distinct layer** under a **distinct anchor** with a **distinct governance body**: NIS 2 (Art. 21(2)(i) `human resources security, access control policies and asset management` — entity-level **partial** asset-management coordination via the access-control sub-domain; entity-discretionary under Recital 56 proportionality; management-body approve/oversee/liable per Art. 20(1)) — **partial coverage only**; CRA (Annex VII §1 `general description` (intended purpose + software versions affecting compliance) + Annex VII §2 `design, development, production and vulnerability-handling processes` (system architecture description documenting how software components build on or feed into each other)) — **partial coverage only**, documentation-centric, not a formal asset register in the DORA sense; **DORA** (Art. 7(1) `Financial entities shall identify, classify and adequately document all ICT supported business functions, the roles and responsibilities supporting those functions, the information assets and ICT assets supporting those functions, and their roles and dependencies in relation to ICT risk` — financial-entity **dedicated asset inventory** with explicit OJ-literal `3-way AND`; Art. 6(4) three-lines-of-defence; Art. 5(2) management-body define/approve/oversee/be responsible) — **the dedicated authority per AEGIS taxonomy §4.1**, OJ-literal.

## Sub-requirements

### 9.3.1 — Entity-level partial asset-management sub-domain coordinated with access control (no dedicated asset-inventory mandate)

```yaml
- req_id: 9.3.1
  volere_section: 15
  volere_subtype: audit

  title: "Essential and important entities adopt asset-management sub-domain coordinated with access control under NIS 2 Art. 21(2)(i), approved and overseen by the management body"

  derives_from:

    objective_ref_section: "### 9.3.1"

  description: |
    The product shall ensure that the essential or important entity adopts access control policies coordinated with human resources security and asset management (per NIS 2 Art. 21(2)(i)), with the management body approving the Art. 21 cybersecurity risk-management measures and overseeing their implementation (per Art. 20(1) sentence 1).

  fit_criterion: |
    The Art. 21(2)(i) sub-domain `human resources security, access control policies and asset management` (SR-NIS2-005) is established as one of three coordinated sub-domains of access control (HR security + access control + asset management) with PR.PS-01 configuration-management dimension (the entity demonstrates ex post that asset-management is established, reviewed, and updated in light of the proportionality factors — degree of exposure + likelihood of incidents + severity per Recital 56) + ID.AM-01 hardware-inventory dimension (entity demonstrates that inventories of hardware managed within the system are maintained, classified, and reviewed) + ID.AM-02 software-services-systems-inventory dimension (entity demonstrates that inventories of software, services, and systems managed are maintained, classified, and reviewed);
```
